tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: We did the video in the local alleyways and social clubs. There were belly dancers, ballroom dancers, monks gowns and an Alsatian called Charlie. It was a bit like The Rolling Thunder Review, but in a retirement town.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: She was a desert artist, and danced around with old ram’s skulls in the video. Later she came out with a guitar and Sang Hank Williams’ Long Gone Lonesome Blues, with all the yodelling parts.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: We knocked at this old woman’s shack and asked if we could film inside. Her name was Jenny Olsen. She let us have the run of the place, no questions asked.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: We told Alan Wills, and he booked @thewildoutdoors on a plane with a super8 camera the next day. That was that, we were doing a video in the Wild West. It was a fun, impulsive time.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: For the video, we were passing through Death Valley on tour with Supergrass. We decided to stop do a video in a town called Randsburg, an old cowboy ghost town with a small jail and a saloon bar.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: We went to a forest near where we live to shoot the video. We’d not long come back from America so we had this kind of gypsy look that we’d picked up along the way. All of our colours seemed to match up with the music, the album cover, the videos. We got everything spot on.

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text:
@thecoralband our mate Joe Fearon used to say ‘it’s a damp dog day’ when it was raining. That’s where the title comes from. 

Most of the lyrics came from a book I had about pirates. I was obsessed with ‘Your Mind and we Belong Together by Love. I was trying to write a tune like that.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: A.D.D. Stands for A Damp Dog Day, which is like a crappy, bad weather day. It was a cold winter. Joe Fearon was at the session in @ElevatorStudios. Joe set up Deltasonic with Alan. They were best mates.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: He was always recommending us albums and movies. I think ADD had some connection to the Dog Day Afternoon film. Joe had loads of sayings like this. We were all standing around saying, who shall it be the confessions of? And he said A DAMP DOG DAY straight off. So it stuck.

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text:
@thecoralband I was trying to write a song Jonny Mathis could sing. It’s a bit throw away really but listening back it’s better than I remembered. 

The jam at the end is great I think Bill came up with that.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: I wanted to write a song like ‘Rockin’ Chair’ by The Band or ‘I’m Only Sleeping’ by The Beatles. Like Secret Kiss, this has a funeral procession feel: death in Toon-town, the knee-bones-connected-to-the-thigh-bone.

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text:
@thecoralband I think we had this one before the first album. Bill and I demoed it in his bedroom. Ian said it sounded like Travis so we shelved it 🤷‍♂️

When we came toward the end of the recording, Alan Wills said, “You haven’t got any singles”, so we revisited this and Pass It On.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: Alan Wills read from ‘Under Milk Wood’ here, which was a big influence on the album. Alan lived in Wales as a kid and loved the book, so it was a great fit. “Sunny Slow.”

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: This is what it’s like walking around a small town on acid, laughing at the lifeboats, Ian’s hands turning to sausages, Barry from Eastenders in the chippy, the roof of every house laughing at you. All the alleyways and secret stairwells and shops unchanged in a hundred years.

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text:
@thecoralband The Walker Brothers would’ve done a great version of this. I stole a lyric off ‘The Soft Sands’ by The Chordettes for the second verse. 

The video for secret kiss is possibly my favourite Coral video. #TimsTwitterListeningParty @Tim_Burgess

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: One of our more magical songs. Could have been written in 1906 or 2040. A song out of time and space. We listened to a lot of Chordettes, Love, eerie ballroom music.

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text: @thecoralband 🤣I was on holiday in Portugal with my girlfriend and we went to this gypsy market where I bought some boots. When we got back to the apartment I had a spliff and wrote it on a little guitar. Not the best song ever but the energy of the band carries it #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text:
.@thecoralband we were on the tour bus somewhere and Bill tought me how to play a Simon and Garfunkel tune on the guitar. Next minute me and Nick were writing Leizah. 

The character was based on a character from ‘The Sun Also Rises’ by Earnest Hemingway.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: It’s definitely got a Cheech & Chong feel. Reminds me of ‘Motorpsycho Nightmare’ by Bob Dylan.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: Such an effortless tune. We were having a good time. It was written on the way to a gig in Birmingham Institute. We learned that clawhammer picking style and thought, “We’ll get so many fucking songs out of this.” (see ‘After The Fair’, ‘Fallin All Around You’, ‘Cobwebs’).

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text: @thecoralband Could be my favourite @thecoralband track. I remember looking at the moon after we played @glastonbury when I was tripping. I thought I’d invented a new chord and Ian said, “Sounds shit to me” and that’s where the title came from. #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: James Skelly
tweets.text:
@thecoralband wrote this on the train in my head without a guitar when I was about 17 that’s why the melody is so pure. 
When we recorded the version for the album Lee had tendinitis so @IanZBroudie played rhythm guitar it has a great feel I love how subtle Bills guitar playing is on this

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: After being home for a while, we decided the next album should be about where we lived; the dark side of small town life. Street parties, silver tea-sets, secret suicides. All the different characters: Leizah, Bill McCai, Captain Cat, ADDD. Under Milk Wood.

tweets.name: The Coral
tweets.text: We recorded Magic and Medicine with @ianzbroudie in the Christmas period of 2002 when the debut hadn’t been out for four months. By January I think we had 35 songs tracked. Then we went straight back out to America and Japan.

tweets.text: Anyways, back to the oath ceremony. Took the oath. Congratulated my fellow immigrants. Got the naturalization certificate. It’s done. I’m officially a citizen of the United States and ready to vote, weeks before the most consequential U.S. election of our lifetimes.

tweets.name: Mehdi Hasan
tweets.text:
Like a lot of journos (humans?) I've said things years ago that I now deeply regret. Chief among them for me is, more than a decade ago, in my 20s, when I wasn't a public figure, I gave a bunch of speeches to students on Islam/extremism. And I said dumb offensive ranty stuff.
2/

tweets.name: Mehdi Hasan
tweets.text: I’m under no illusions btw: I say this as someone who has been lambasted by many liberals online - & almost hounded out of a job - for having expressed offensive & illiberal views in the past on everything from homosexuality to abortion (views which I regret, & no longer hold).

tweets.name: Mehdi Hasan
tweets.text:
My dad is an engineer from Hyderabad, India. These stories are so hard to read. RIP. 

My thoughts and prayers and solidarity goes out to all the victims’ families.

tweets.name: Mehdi Hasan
tweets.text:
I'm the proud son of south Indian immigrants to the West myself so, yes, let me take some pride in this historic success of the daughter of a south Indian immigrant.

And, no, it doesn't mean I agree with the new VP's policies or positions on any particular issue.

Grow up, folks

tweets.name: The Charlatans
tweets.text:
The album cover is Pickmere Lake, Cheshire. Just outside of Northwich. 

#TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
Apples and Oranges 
I was not a happy lad when I wrote the words for this but I think the words are decent #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
Try Again Today

Written in LA with Linus of Hollywood who co wrote my album I Believe. 
We recorded it and re-recorded it so many times trying to get the vibe of the demo 
Ken Nelson got the best out of us as did @jimspencermusic (as always) #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: The Charlatans
tweets.text:
Track 10 - Loving You Is Easy

I was with @TonyCharlatan before he left Walsall for Ireland. Tony insisted on the vocal swap halfway trough. Love the lyrics on this. We were demo-ing and Tony just started jamming at the end. He wanted it cutting. We insisted it stayed.

tweets.name: Tony Rogers
tweets.text: Minnie Ripperton has a song by the same title,  #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
It was hard trying to keep the band and my marriage together - think this song reveals that to me now. 
So much travelling, so much drinking, so many time differences. Saying that, this is one of my favourite songs on the album #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
I’ll Sing A Hymn (You Came To Me) 
I am not really sure what I think of this one. 
I was channelling Please Don’t Leave Me Lonely by King Floyd but I am not sure if it strikes the chords I wanted to strike #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
Blue For you
Written about Rob. Cathartic? Yes, 
but did it need to go on the album? Let’s see #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tony Rogers
tweets.text:
Cry yourself to sleep, 
Bit of a sad song really, Daughters emigrated to Australia, they used to cry a lot cuz they didn’t want to leave.  #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
Cry Yourself To Sleep
Tony had a ton of songs this was one of them. Melody and words. 
I might have added a few words - but I don’t think I did 
Tony should maybe have sung this one, like Loving You Is Easy #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text: I was very inspired it seems by When I Paint My Masterpiece by Bob Dylan #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
As I Watch You in Disbelief

It’s a story of being broken is it boigraphical? Yes #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: The Charlatans
tweets.text:
Track 10 - Try Again Today

Great song. Always thought the finish on this version is slightly overproduced. A chap called Mike Cave helped mix the first version. That version is on Forever: The Singles (the expanded version)

#TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: The Charlatans
tweets.text:
Track 2 - Feel The Pressure

Mark came up with the original music. The Chemical Brothers did their thing to it. The original band version is probably more in-keeping with the album. Both are great.

#TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:
Feel the pressure

Full horror movie intro provided by our long time collaborators @ChemBros #TimsTwitterListeningParty

tweets.name: Tim Burgess
tweets.text:

I can't help but think his process is way cooler than mine.

#amwriting

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
Get ready for Earth joy!

Earlier today, we announced the four astronauts who will go to space on Artemis III —and shared the latest updates on the future of @NASAArtemis. 
Learn more about how this mission will set us up to return humanity to the Moon: https://t.co/kAvzvuYzEw https://t.co/taD2lCdYiC

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
Introducing Artemis III.

Four astronauts. Three launches. Two dockings. One splashdown.

In 2027, the Artemis III mission will practice docking the Orion spacecraft with two lunar landers in low Earth orbit — the capability we need to return humanity to the Moon’s surface. https://t.co/8uhMUxuuWX

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
“We just want to say how completely fired up we are for you.”

Artemis II crew members @Astro_Reid, @AstroVicGlover, and @Astro_Christina hand the Artemis baton to the newly-announced Artemis III crew. https://t.co/nYwnHKwnqv

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
“[NASA’s] collective effort will lift all of us up on that SLS rocket when we hit launch day.”

Artemis III commander @AstroKomrade reflects on the path that led to here, and how this mission will lay the path for all future Artemis missions. https://t.co/251Nu5Jq67

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
“[My] launchpad is my country, Italy…The European Space Agency is like a launch tower… the rocket, literally and figuratively, is NASA.” 

@ESA astronaut and Artemis III pilot @Astro_Luca shares words of gratitude. https://t.co/a3w2AsCZcs

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
“What an incredible blessing and an honor it is to be standing here representing all of you.” 

Artemis III mission specialist Frank Rubio takes a moment to thank his family and colleagues for their support. https://t.co/zHqW5ipx9m

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
“My brain is going a mile a minute right now, but my heart, it is so warm. It is so full.”

Artemis III mission specialist @Astro_AndreD reflects on his journey and the people in his life who have supported him along the way. https://t.co/LMgJYO9BXg

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text: Introducing your Artemis III crew: NASA astronauts @AstroKomrade, @Astro_AndreD, and Frank Rubio and @ESA astronaut @Astro_Luca. https://t.co/ljfxlOBw0U

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text: Now for our Artemis III commander: two-time astronaut, former @Space_Station commander, @USMC colonel, and test pilot @AstroKomrade. https://t.co/iySkrD9h4s

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text: Our Artemis III pilot is @Astro_Luca: Two-time flyer, @ESA astronaut, and the first Italian @Space_Station commander. https://t.co/W3H1UoLYdo

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text: .@USArmy Black Hawk helicopter pilot, family medicine physician, and record-setting astronaut Frank Rubio is joining Artemis III as a mission specialist. https://t.co/3Uoz1ZbZG4

tweets.text: .@USCG Reserve commander @Astro_AndreD is making his first spaceflight as Artemis III mission specialist. https://t.co/HPu0zrxxNO

tweets.text: Introducing our Artemis III backup crew member: Test pilot, Air Force colonel, and @SpaceX Crew-4 pilot @Astro_FarmerBob. https://t.co/bX4rMwbIBh

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
“While Artemis II was all about Moon Joy, Artemis III will be all about Earth Joy.”

tweets.name: NASA
tweets.text:
"Artemis III is an incredibly exciting, complicated, and highly coordinated multi-launch campaign. It’s going to happen in a short period of time with three of the world’s most powerful rockets."

Artemis lead Jeremy Parsons describes exactly what Artemis III will entail. https://t.co/kbaLeN5l8U

quoted.text: Technology must serve humanity. Our Real Time Governance System (RTGS) is transforming lives across Andhra Pradesh - delivering speed in governance & ease of doing business in real time. tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: I’m deeply sad to learn of the passing of Dr. Bill Foege. Bill was a towering figure in global health—a man who saved the lives of literally hundreds of millions of people. He was also a friend and mentor who gave me a deep grounding in the history of global health and inspired

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: Learning how many kids were dying from diarrhea back in 1997—when I never worried about it with my own kids—led me to get involved in global health. https://t.co/NYitqiRY38

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: Thanks to vaccines, fewer children are dying from preventable diseases around the world. Cutting funding for them would reverse that progress and put millions of children in danger. https://t.co/UF7IaASepq

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: The devastating effects of these cuts are entirely preventable—and it’s not too late to reverse them.

quoted.name: Sam Stein
quoted.text:
An HIV doctor in Africa, whose work depends on USAID and PEPFAR, sends in a dispatch of the bleak situation now unfolding there 

https://t.co/Pvo239Fh7u https://t.co/zEhK2CsXqJ

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: The facts are simple and devastating: Aid cuts have already cost lives, and the number of deaths will continue to rise. Here’s the evidence.

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: To me, this is the most remarkable thing about global health: With a relatively small amount of money, you can do a great deal of good for a great many people. This is money well spent, and we should go back to spending it—now.

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: Health aid is a small piece of America’s foreign aid, which is itself a small piece of the federal budget. In 2023, the US spent less than one percent of the federal budget on lifesaving global health programs: https://t.co/QaTuRs6PQe https://t.co/La7oeJWJfh

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: According to a @UNAIDS analysis, ending PEPFAR-supported programs for people living with HIV could result in an additional 4.2 million deaths by 2029: https://t.co/YjBtRcxNzH https://t.co/31LylESN5C

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: Since 2000, @gavi has helped get vaccines to 1.1 billion children. The U.S. announced that, after this year, it’s pulling out all its money. If that happens, Gavi estimates that 75 million children will miss vaccinations over the next five years—and of those, 1.2 million children https://t.co/S1hkx8QH73

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: A study in the Lancet recently looked at the cumulative impact of reductions in American aid. It found that, by 2040, 8 million more children will die before their fifth birthday: https://t.co/HBeCIFXTDY

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: U.S. global health and development aid is estimated to have saved 92 million lives over the past 20 years. We can't afford to let that kind of progress unravel: https://t.co/eqLEVzB9gA https://t.co/4JJJfyO2E3

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: There’s never been a point in the past 25 years when more lives hung in the balance. https://t.co/q8I2dBdtYB

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: It's not too late to change course.

quoted.name: USGLC
quoted.text: Happening now at the Senate Approps hearing on rescissions: @SenatorCollins (R-ME) says PEPFAR "remains a bipartisan priority of Congress" and emphasizes that cutting "funding aimed at preventing disease transmission would be extraordinarily ill-advised and short-sighted." https://t.co/ZuT0zLxU3s

tweets.name: Bill Gates
tweets.text: In the last 25 years, @gavi has helped over a billion kids live better, healthier lives. Pulling the organization’s funding would have devastating consequences: more sick kids who fall behind in school, more overcrowded hospital wards, and eventually more grieving parents. I’m

quoted.name: Mark Suzman
quoted.text:
Today, world leaders reaffirmed their commitment to protecting children from vaccine-preventable diseases, raising $9 billion for @gavi. This reflects strong global confidence in vaccines as one of the most effective investments in global health.

As a founding partner, the

How do you set up a passkey?

➡️ Head to https://t.co/skMY3mWOLH → security & sign-in → passkeys & security keys → create a passkey.

tweets.name: Google
tweets.text:
Does using a passkey mean Google has access to my personal information?

❌ No. Your biometric data, such as a fingerprint or face scan, is stored on your personal device and never shared with Google.

tweets.name: Google
tweets.text:
Are passkeys more secure than traditional passwords? 

✅ Yes. Because no one can guess or reuse your passkey — it’s specific to you and your biometric data — it’s the most secure way to sign in to your devices.

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
Congrats, @grahamformaine! Maine Democrats chose a fighter with the grit to take on billionaires and giant corporations and WIN. 

This grassroots movement is just getting started. On to November—let's flip this seat and take back the Senate!

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
Trump cut nearly a trillion dollars from Medicaid to pay for tax cuts for giant corporations.

Now they're telling cancer patients and people with HIV that they need to work harder to keep their health insurance.

Unconscionable. https://t.co/b0fiw9dA22

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
AI data centers are doubling electricity demand.

Private equity executives see a cash grab.

They're buying up utility companies to cash in on the energy surge and pass costs to consumers while making services worse.

I’m fighting back.

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
For years, CEOs have sold a story that a $15 minimum wage would kill jobs.

The Washington establishment bought this trickle-down lie, and workers paid the price.

But the evidence debunks that myth.

It's long past time to raise the minimum wage. https://t.co/a1OQZ2Icc0

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
People are realizing that you can’t lower healthcare costs without tackling corporate greed in the healthcare system.

It’s time we finally rein in the healthcare middlemen jacking up drug costs and driving small pharmacies out of business.

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
Acting AG Todd Blanche—formerly Donald Trump's personal lawyer—signed an order blocking the IRS from investigating the President, his family, and his businesses.

This may be valued at over $100 million.

I have a bill to stop this corruption.

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text: Congress and the American people deserve answers about whether the Trump White House is rigging or intervening in Pentagon contracting decisions to benefit the President’s family. https://t.co/FSx72jppok

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
The AI moment means we have to step up.

We can't wait a few more years to see how many people get laid off, how many lives get turned upside down.

This is the moment for Congress to take action.

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
Should Saudi, Qatari, & Abu Dhabi wealth funds have a near-majority stake in one of America's largest media companies?

The Paramount-Warner Bros. deal could mean foreign access to your personal data—and leverage over U.S. media.

Block the merger.

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
Trump Jr. invests in a company.

A Trump White House staffer reportedly calls the Pentagon.

The Pentagon rushes a $620M loan through in weeks.
The company's value jumps tenfold.

Congress must investigate NOW. https://t.co/KwIwJnHWbB

tweets.text: Americans deserve to know: who is ICE actually working for? https://t.co/b7dSuPJ8uC

tweets.name: Elizabeth Warren
tweets.text:
Trump promised you cheap energy.

Instead, he launched a war with Iran that handed oil companies the biggest windfall in years.

Now working families are paying the price.
